summary: Focused re-test of two guided-ritual bug fixes on preview URL. All 4 test scenarios PASSED. (1) Sparse guided result (ZIP 83012, radius 1mi) still renders a FULL 5-card stacked shuffling deck (count=5) and lands on a valid result card (Dornan's Chuckwagon, Moose WY). (2) Enter key on guided ZIP input blurs the field AND advances to the chips step. (3) Tapping the dark backdrop (top-left, outside step card) blurs the focused ZIP input and leaves the step-card intact (no crash). (4) Regression: classic Deal Your Fate with ZIP 10001 still shows a full 5-card shuffling deck and lands on a result (Altair Restaurant NYC).
critical_code_review_comments: ShufflingDeck padding logic in Home.jsx correctly cycles source[i % source.length] to pad to DECK_SIZE(5); handles empty source gracefully (renders 0 cards, no crash).
GuidedFlow.jsx onKeyDown Enter uses e.target.blur() then calls next() only if ZIP is valid (5-digit) — good guard. Backdrop onMouseDown blurs only INPUT elements, so it won't fight button clicks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified: shuffling-deck always shows 5 cards even for tiny result pools (padded by cycling). Enter on ZIP input blurs+advances. Backdrop mousedown blurs input without crashing step. To replay guided flow, clear localStorage 'ff_ritual_done' and reload. Slider min via keyboard: focus [role=slider] then ArrowLeft ~50x -> 1 mi. ZIP 83012 (Moose, WY) with 1 mi radius produces sparse (~1 result) pool that used to expose the single-card bug.
